mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 2.5% indicates persistent inflationary pressures, prompting central bank tightening and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in < 40% of triggered instances, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and declines in unemployment (UNRATE) signal a tightening labor market, which drives corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of PAYEMS and UNRATE on SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in any 12-month rolling window, or the interaction term's coefficient is less than 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) during a supply-side disruption regime signals reduced corporate output and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances when INDPRO declines, or the Rank IC falls below 0.03 in any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong demand for residential construction, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in < 40% of instances when HOUST rises, or the Rank IC is below 0.02 in any 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly RSAFS value above 430,000 million dollars indicates strong housing demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in < 40% of instances when RSAFS exceeds 430,000, or the Rank IC is below 0.03 in any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) and low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS < 1%) signals a central bank's accommodative stance despite inflation, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS on SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in any 12-month rolling window, or the interaction term's coefficient is less than 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) combined with low unemployment (UNRATE) signals overheating labor markets, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in < 40% of instances during late-cycle expansions, or the interaction term's coefficient is less than 0.02.
